categories.title: INTERNET COUNTRY CODES
categories.theme: Questions ask which region of the world uses a specific country-code top-level domain (ccTLD); answers are the countries/regions

clues.clue: The '.uk' country-code top-level domain belongs to this island nation west of continental Europe.
clues.fit_justification: Source question literally asks 'Which region of the world uses ".uk" at the end of its web addresses?' and source answer is 'The United Kingdom'.

clues.clue: The code '.va' is the internet country code for this tiny sovereign state inside Rome, home to the Pope.
clues.fit_justification: Source question literally asks 'Which region of the world uses ".va" at the end of its web addresses?' and source answer is 'Vatican City'.

clues.clue: The internet country code '.za' is assigned to this nation at the southern tip of the African continent.
clues.fit_justification: Source question literally asks 'Which region of the world uses ".za" at the end of its web addresses?' and source answer is 'South Africa'.

clues.clue: The '.sd' top-level domain belongs to this northeastern African country whose name references a directional compass point.
clues.fit_justification: Source question literally asks 'Which region of the world uses ".sd" at the end of its web addresses?' and source answer is 'Sudan'.

clues.clue: Central America's '.ni' internet country code belongs to this nation whose capital is Managua.
clues.fit_justification: Source question literally asks 'Which region of the world uses ".ni" at the end of its web addresses?' and source answer is 'Nicaragua'.

categories.title: RIVERS & MOUNTAINS
categories.theme: Questions ask about specific rivers or mountain ranges and where they are located or what they are called

clues.fit_justification: Source question literally asks 'The ancient city of Mohenjo-daro lies along which river in Pakistan?' and source answer is 'The Indus', a river.

categories.title: CAPITAL CITIES
categories.theme: Each clue asks for or identifies a capital city; the answer is that capital city

CORRECTION: source answer is Netherlands; Amsterdam IS confirmed as the capital in the question text. Clue asks which city, answer Amsterdam is named in the source question as the capital.

clues.clue: The UNESCO World Heritage site of Cappadocia, known for its striking 'fairy chimney' rock formations, lies in this country.
clues.fit_justification: Source question asks 'In which country would you find the UNESCO World Heritage site of Cappadocia?' A: Turkey — country identification fact.

finals.title: DOUBLY LANDLOCKED
finals.clue: Of the world's only two doubly landlocked countries — surrounded entirely by other landlocked countries — this Central Asian nation is the larger one by population, with its capital at Tashkent.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which of these countries is "doubly landlocked" (surrounded entirely by one or more landlocked countries)?' and source answer is 'Uzbekistan', a doubly landlocked nation.

finals.title: FAMOUS LANDMARKS
finals.clue: Known locally as 'The Coathanger,' this iconic steel arch bridge spans the largest natural harbour in the world and opened in 1932.
finals.fit_justification: Source question: 'Which Famous City Bridge Is Known Locally As The Coathanger?' Source answer: 'Sydney Harbour Bridge'. The source question literally contains the nickname 'Coathanger' and the answer is a named landmark.

finals.title: GEOGRAPHY FINAL
finals.clue: Derived from an Indigenous word meaning 'the edge of Tibet,' this small Himalayan kingdom borders India and China and is famous for measuring national happiness rather than GDP.
finals.fit_justification: Source question states the name 'is derived from the Indian word Bhotanta, meaning the edge of Tibet' and that it is located in Asia near the eastern Himalayas; source answer is 'Bhutan'.

finals.title: GEOGRAPHY FINAL
finals.clue: Described by the French as 'la Manche' — meaning 'the sleeve' — this body of water separates southern England from northern France and connects the North Sea to the Atlantic Ocean.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'What geographical feature is referred to as la Manche (the sleeve) by the French?' and source answer is 'The English Channel'.

finals.title: WORLD WONDERS
finals.clue: Serving as the last tributary of the world's longest river, the Atbarah River joins this waterway in northern Sudan before it flows on to the Mediterranean.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which river is joined by the Atbarah River in northern Sudan as its last tributary?' and the answer is 'The Nile'; the clue also echoes the world's-longest-river fact from source otdb:7xatde, making this a satisfying final with double geographic grounding.

finals.title: LANDLOCKED NATIONS
finals.clue: This southern African country is entirely surrounded by the borders of a single neighboring nation, making it one of only three enclaved countries in the world.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'Which of the following landlocked countries is entirely contained within another country?' and source answer is 'Lesotho', a geographically remarkable enclave nation.

finals.title: GEOGRAPHY FINAL
finals.clue: This narrow strip of land connects North America and South America and gives its name to a famous canal completed in 1914.
finals.fit_justification: Source question asks 'What is the land connecting North America and South America?' and source answer is 'Isthmus of Panama'; question text literally describes the geographic feature connecting the two continents.

finals.title: GEOGRAPHY FINAL
finals.clue: Known as 'China's Sorrow,' this river has caused catastrophic floods throughout Chinese history and is the second longest river in the country.
finals.fit_justification: Source question 'What river is known as China's sorrow because of its flooding?' and source answer 'The Yellow River' — niche geographic nickname makes this ideal for a Final clue.
